claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week but is in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education

Child is not in local authority care

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, I need to ask you a few questions about your situation. Let me begin:

**What is the name of the child (or children) you're inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wondering if I'm eligible. I have one child - his name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child named Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to gather some more details about your circumstances.

**My first question: How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Before I can give you a final answer, I need to check the specific eligibility rules. Let me review the official guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can see that Alex is 19 years old. According to the rules, normally you'd qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in **approved education or training**.

So my next question is:

**Is Alex currently enrolled in or attending approved education or training?**

This could include things like school, college, university, or approved apprenticeships or training schemes.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's doing his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that information. That's helpful to know that Alex is in approved education.

I need to ask one more question to complete my assessment:

**Are you responsible for Alex and living with him in the UK?** 

This means either you live with Alex or you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after him - for example on food, clothes or pocket money.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility.

Based on the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules:

- Alex is 19 years old and is currently in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- You live in the UK with Alex
- You are responsible for Alex

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. However, **you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.**

Since Alex meets these criteria, I can now provide you with my eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:
- Alex is 19 years old, which is under the age limit of 20
- Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college)
- You live in the UK with Alex and are responsible for him

All of these conditions are met, so you qualify for Child Benefit. I'd recommend applying through the official Child Benefit application process if you haven't already done so.

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and currently in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK with Alex and is responsible for him. Therefore, Alex meets all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is under 20, in approved education, and the claimant is responsible for and lives with Alex in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 19 years old and currently in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives in the UK with Alex and is responsible for him. Therefore, Alex meets all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is under 20, in approved education, and the claimant is responsible for and lives with Alex in the UK. All eligibility criteria are met.
